Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Long Overlooked as Crucial to Life, Fungi Start to Get Their Due 40 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 239 upvotes, 38 comments
  3. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 27 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Italian prosecutors seek trial for Amazon, 4 execs in alleged $1.4B tax evasion 148 upvotes, 29 comments
  5. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 76 upvotes, 13 comments
  6. DDR4 Sdram – Initialization, Training and Calibration 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  7. The Met Releases High-Def 3D Scans of 140 Famous Art Objects 113 upvotes, 25 comments
  8. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 187 upvotes, 52 comments
  9. Full Spectrum and Infrared Photography 23 upvotes, 3 comments
  10. Contextual commits – An open standard for capturing the why in Git history 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  11. The Cost of Indirection in Rust 53 upvotes, 13 comments
  12. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 637 upvotes, 249 comments
  13. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 199 upvotes, 73 comments
  14. Show HN: Understudy – Teach a desktop agent by demonstrating a task once 36 upvotes, 9 comments
  15. Apple's MacBook Neo makes repairs easier and cheaper than other MacBooks 73 upvotes, 26 comments
  16. Show HN: Aurion OS – A 32-bit GUI operating system written from scratch in C 14 upvotes, 2 comments
  17. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 100 upvotes, 42 comments
  18. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 59 upvotes, 25 comments
  19. Claude now creates interactive charts, diagrams and visualizations 108 upvotes, 54 comments
  20. Show HN: Web-based ANSI art viewer 13 upvotes, 3 comments
  21. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 51 upvotes, 24 comments
  22. Asia rolls out 4-day weeks, WFH to solve fuel crisis caused by Iran war 270 upvotes, 171 comments
  23. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B (2025) 191 upvotes, 120 comments
  24. Show HN: Rudel – Claude Code Session Analytics 107 upvotes, 63 comments
  25. Kotlin creator's new language: a formal way to talk to LLMs instead of English 206 upvotes, 169 comments
  26. Show HN: Axe – A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 69 upvotes, 57 comments
  27. ATMs didn't kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 165 upvotes, 212 comments
  28. The Road Not Taken: A World Where IPv4 Evolved 23 upvotes, 23 comments
  29. Scrt: A CLI secret manager for developers, sysadmins and DevOps 6 upvotes, 6 comments
  30. Converge (YC S23) Is Hiring a Founding Platform Engineer (NYC, Onsite) 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 3:00am, 13 March 2026. Source here.
